programme.sub-title: We celebrate the greatest goalscorers in Premier League history; the icons whose instinct, power, and flair have lit up stadiums and etched their names into footballing folklore. programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: (Subbed) BRUNO GUIMARÃES
programme.sub-title: We sit down with Bruno Guimarães to trace his remarkable journey from the streets of Brazil to Premier League stardom with Newcastle United. Travelling back to South America, we meet the family, friends, and coaches who shaped his early years and believed in his dream. Bruno opens up about the sacrifices, setbacks, and defining moments that fuelled his rise, offering a heartfelt look at the person behind the player.

programme.title: Review of the Season 2024/2025 (LIV)
programme.sub-title: Relive another extraordinary Premier League campaign as Liverpool soared to an historic 20th league title, Nottingham Forest defied expectations and the promoted sides were strenuously challenged. There were also tactical transformations, shifts in narrative trends, and a more open field than ever before. Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.

programme.sub-title: We catch up with Juan Mata in Australia, where the former Premier League star is now playing with Western Sydney Wanderers. As he begins a new chapter down under, he also opens up about life after the Premier League and what lies ahead both on and off the pitch.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP07: BRAD FRIEDEL
programme.sub-title: Arguably the greatest American goalkeeper of all time, Brad Friedel made an incredible 450 appearances in the Premier League playing for Tottenham, Aston Villa, Blackburn and Liverpool. We sit down with the 52-year-old to recount his incredible journey to the top of English football.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP48: (Subbed) REGGAE RHYTHMS IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE
programme.sub-title: Reggae Rhythms in the Premier League explores the powerful connection between Jamaica and the world’s most-watched football league, showcasing how the island’s culture, music, and style have influenced the Premier League. We feature iconic Jamaican players who have left their mark, while also diving into the impact of reggae music, fashion, and fan culture on the game. Through exclusive interviews and untold stories, we see how Jamaican heritage has shaped both football and broader global culture, from the pitch to the streets.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP08: KEEP IT IN THE FAMILY
programme.sub-title: We celebrate the unique legacy of footballing dynasties in the Premier League; where talent, passion, and pride are passed from one generation to the next. From the iconic saves of Peter Schmeichel to the rise of his son Kasper, the goal-scoring legacy of Ian Wright carried on by son’s Shaun and Bradley, and the Lampards' deep-rooted Chelsea connection, this episode explores the bonds that tie football families together. Through exclusive interviews and emotional reflections, we uncover how the Premier League has become a stage not just for individual greatness, but for generational stories written in the beautiful game.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP26: THE GREATEST GAME EVER PLAYED
programme.sub-title: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? Join two ex-footballers, a seasoned commentator, and a top football journalist as they lock horns in a passionate roundtable discussion. The panel revisits iconic moments, dissecting tactics, and legendary performances. Expect banter, bold claims, and maybe even a few surprises, as they strive to settle the debate—or perhaps keep it alive. Whether reliving “Fergie Time” magic, Leicester’s miracle season, or Aguero’s legendary finish, this episode celebrates the unfiltered drama that only Premier League history can offer.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP15: SHAY GIVEN: MY NEWCASTLE YEARS
programme.sub-title: Renowned for his lightning-fast reflexes and unwavering courage, Shay Given is one of the Premier League’s all-time greatest goalkeepers. The Irishman reflects on his most memorable moments between the posts as Newcastle’s number one.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP12: JERMAIN DEFOE: MY TOTTENHAM YEARS
programme.sub-title: Jermain Defoe scored 162 Premier League goals, and it was at Tottenham where he was at his prolific best. The striker recalls his two spells with the North London club, including the memorable occasion where he hit 5 in one match.
programme.desc: Jermain Defoe scored 162 Premier League goals, and it was at Tottenham where he was at his prolific best. The striker recalls his two spells with the North London club, including the memorable occasion where he hit 5 in one match.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP42: (Subbed) NEURODIVERSITY CELEBRATION
programme.sub-title: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. Through personal stories, including those of Joao Gomes (stutter), Steven Naismith (dyslexia), Greg Halford (autism), and Charlie Austin (ADHD), the show celebrates resilience and the positive impact neurodiverse traits bring to the football world. Tim's own journey intertwines with these stories, showcasing the triumphs that come from embracing neurodiversity.
programme.desc: In this PL Stories show, Tim Howard, who has neurodiverse conditions, leads a conversation with past and present players, exploring the challenges and strengths of living with neurodiversity. programme.title: PL STORIES EP51: GOODBYE GOODISON
programme.sub-title: As the curtain comes down on Everton’s time at Goodison Park, PL Stories takes a poignant look back at one of English football’s most iconic grounds. For over 130 years, Goodison has been more than just a stadium; it’s been a home, a fortress, and the stage for countless moments of triumph, heartbreak, and unforgettable drama. Through the voices of players, fans, and historians, we relive the stories etched into the blue brick walls; from title-winning glory to relegation battles, Merseyside derbies to European nights. This is a tribute to the legacy of Goodison Park, and the soul of a club that has lived and breathed within its stands.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP52: CHAMPIONS STORY
programme.sub-title: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. As Liverpool draw level with United’s record haul of top-flight titles, this is a story of history, pride, and the relentless pursuit of greatness. With insight from icons on both sides—Ian Rush, Lou Macari, Brian Kidd, Michael Owen, Phil Thompson, and new Liverpool boss Arne Slot, we unpack the moments that have defined generations, split families, and lit up the Premier League. From legendary goals to seismic title races, this is the story of two giants locked in a battle for supremacy. Woven through it all is the significance of Liverpool’s record-equalling achievement—a milestone that adds a powerful new chapter to football’s greatest rivalry.
programme.desc: We dive into the fiercest rivalry in English football—Manchester United and Liverpool. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP42: CHAMPIONS SPECIAL WITH CURTIS JONES
programme.sub-title: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.
programme.desc: With the remnants of the celebratory confetti barely gone from the Anfield pitch, Curtis Jones joins the Big Interview to reflect on an historic title-winning season. Having signed for his beloved team at just 9 years of age, the Liverpool born and bred midfielder explains just how much the title triumph means to him and sheds light on the impact of Arne Slot and the differences in approach of his new manager compared to Jurgen Klopp. In a year where Jones also enjoyed a goal-scoring senior debut for England and became a father for the first time, there's been plenty for him to celebrate.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP09: NEWCASTLE UNITED - NOT IN MY LIFETIME
programme.sub-title: Newcastle United are a team on the rise under Eddie Howe; a manager quietly rewriting the modern history of the club. With exclusive access and interviews from inside St. James’ Park, we uncover the transformation Howe has led, from stabilising the team to delivering silverware and reigniting belief on Tyneside. As the Magpies set their sights on even greater heights, we ask just how far he can take this ambitious, resurgent club.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP05: MANAGER RIVALRIES
programme.sub-title: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. From the fierce mind games between Arsène Wenger and Sir Alex Ferguson, to the emotional explosion of Kevin Keegan’s title-chasing Newcastle. From the simmering tension and touchline clashes of Mourinho vs. Wenger, to the tactical chess match between Jürgen Klopp and Pep Guardiola. Through exclusive interviews and unforgettable moments, we explore how these rivalries pushed the limits and left a lasting mark on the Premier League.
programme.desc: We peel back the layers on the Premier League’s most iconic managerial rivalries; battles that shaped eras, defined clubs, and captivated fans around the world. From the fierce mind games between Arsène Wenger and Sir Alex Ferguson, to the emotional explosion of Kevin Keegan’s title-chasing Newcastle. From the simmering tension and touchline clashes of Mourinho vs. Wenger, to the tactical chess match between Jürgen Klopp and Pep Guardiola. Through exclusive interviews and unforgettable moments, we explore how these rivalries pushed the limits and left a lasting mark on the Premier League.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP08: KEEP IT IN THE FAMILY
programme.sub-title: We celebrate the unique legacy of footballing dynasties in the Premier League; where talent, passion, and pride are passed from one generation to the next. From the iconic saves of Peter Schmeichel to the rise of his son Kasper, the goal-scoring legacy of Ian Wright carried on by son’s Shaun and Bradley, and the Lampards' deep-rooted Chelsea connection, this episode explores the bonds that tie football families together. Through exclusive interviews and emotional reflections, we uncover how the Premier League has become a stage not just for individual greatness, but for generational stories written in the beautiful game.
programme.desc: We celebrate the unique legacy of footballing dynasties in the Premier League; where talent, passion, and pride are passed from one generation to the next. programme.sub-title: The very best from the 2024/25 season as Hollywood superstar Michael B Jordan tries to guess what happens next when he watches some infamous PL moments. Emi Martinez ranks his best saves while Liverpool legend John Barnes answers some burning questions.
